items.title: Peer Justice
items.description: A student-led initiative that promotes conflict resolution and restorative justice within the school community. Trained youth leaders help their peers navigate disputes through open dialogue and collective problem-solving.
features: Creates safer, more inclusive school environments

items.title: The Circle (sponsored by Teen Court)
items.description: A flexible alternative to traditional juvenile justice that empowers youth to take responsibility and make better decisions. Activities are tailored to each situation to interrupt harmful patterns and keep youth out of the justice system.
features: Traditional Teen Court with peer jury
RJ Circle or conference with person harmed
RJ Circle or conference with school

items.title: Restorative Circles & Conferences
items.description: Structured conversations that bring together those affected by conflict or harm. Circles are broader and community-inclusive; conferences are more formal and targeted to a specific incident. Both are guided by a trained facilitator.
features: Person who caused harm and person harmed
Facilitated dialogue with a talking piece
Focus on inclusion, accountability, and repair

items.title: ESP — Evaluation & Support Program
items.description: An alternative to expulsion and traditional juvenile justice sanctions. ESP uses positive discipline and connects youth and families with community resources to reduce disparities in discipline and support students returning to school.
features: Connects youth to mental health and community supports
